title: lithospermum cinereum
intro: Lithospermum cinereum, also known as Glaucous Puccoon or Rock Gentian, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, blue-violet flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Lithospermum cinereum has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and natural remedies.
